name: Augmented Reality (ARKit + RealityKit)
promptBlock: AR Ruler / measurement apps: Never place measurement points randomly on tap position alone. The correct pattern is: (1) Show a persistent crosshair or reticle in the exact center of the screen at all times using a SwiftUI overlay. (2) On each tap, place the measurement point at the world position that the center reticle is currently aimed at — use a raycast from the screen center (CGPoint(x: arView.bounds.midX, y: arView.bounds.midY)) not from the tap location. (3) First tap sets point A, second tap sets point B, then show the measurement. (4) Render the two endpoints as small flat discs or dots (not 3D spheres/marbles) so they don't obscure the measurement line. (5) Show the measurement label floating above the midpoint of the line, not overlapping it. (6) Show a Reset button to clear and start over.
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Augmented Reality (ARKit + RealityKit)

ARKit requires UIKit bridging via UIViewRepresentable — it does NOT have native SwiftUI views.

IMPORTS (CRITICAL):
- `import ARKit` for AR session, anchors, and configuration.
- `import RealityKit` for ARView, Entity, ModelEntity, and 3D content.
- `import UIKit` is REQUIRED in any file using UIViewRepresentable, UIView, or UIViewController — SwiftUI does NOT implicitly import UIKit.
- `import Combine` if using Publishers for AR session events.

UIViewRepresentable PATTERN (CORRECT):
```
struct ARViewContainer: UIViewRepresentable {
    typealias UIViewType = ARView

    func makeUIView(context: Context) -> ARView {
        let arView = ARView(frame: .zero)
        let config = ARWorldTrackingConfiguration()
        config.planeDetection = [.horizontal]
        arView.session.run(config)
        return arView
    }

    func updateUIView(_ uiView: ARView, context: Context) {}

    func makeCoordinator() -> Coordinator { Coordinator() }

    class Coordinator: NSObject, ARSessionDelegate {}
}
```

CRITICAL UIViewRepresentable RULES:
- The `makeUIView(context:)` method signature MUST use `Context` (which is `UIViewRepresentableContext<Self>`). Do NOT write `UIViewRepresentableContext<ARViewContainer>` explicitly — just use `Context`.
- The `updateUIView(_:context:)` method MUST match: first param is the UIView type, second is `Context`.
- The UIView type must match `typealias UIViewType`.
- If you need the coordinator, access `context.coordinator`.

AR MEASURING:
- Use ARRaycastQuery to find real-world positions from screen taps.
- `arView.raycast(from:allowing:alignment:)` returns [ARRaycastResult].
- Distance: `simd_distance(pointA.columns.3, pointB.columns.3)` between transforms.
- Display distance with a floating text entity or SwiftUI overlay.

3D CONTENT:
- Simple shapes: `ModelEntity(mesh: .generateBox(size: 0.1), materials: [SimpleMaterial(color: .blue, isMetallic: false)])`
- Place at anchor: `let anchor = AnchorEntity(plane: .horizontal); anchor.addChild(model); arView.scene.addAnchor(anchor)`
- Animate: use `entity.move(to:relativeTo:duration:)` for transform animations.

VERTICAL PLANES (WALLS) — interior design, wall paint, art frames:
- Use planeDetection = [.horizontal, .vertical] so walls are detected. Using only .horizontal will not detect vertical surfaces.
- Wall paint (correct pattern — entity must lie flat on the wall, not stick out like a shelf): (1) On tap, raycast with arView.raycast(from: location, allowing: .existingPlaneGeometry, alignment: .vertical). (2) Take result.worldTransform. (3) Create the wall box: ModelEntity(mesh: .generateBox(width: 1.5, height: 1.5, depth: 0.01), materials: [SimpleMaterial(color: UIColor(selectedColor).withAlphaComponent(0.4), isMetallic: false)]). (4) Create AnchorEntity(world: transform). (5) Rotate the entity 90° around X so the flat face points toward the camera: wallEntity.transform.rotation = simd_quatf(angle: .pi / 2, axis: SIMD3(1, 0, 0)). (6) anchor.addChild(wallEntity). (7) arView.scene.addAnchor(anchor). Do NOT use AnchorEntity(plane: .vertical) for wall paint — it does not orient correctly and can appear as a floating horizontal shelf. The 90° X rotation is required when using AnchorEntity(world:) so the thin box face (depth 0.01) lies flush on the wall.
- Picture frame on wall: same raycast (allowing: .existingPlaneGeometry, alignment: .vertical), take result.worldTransform, create frame ModelEntity (e.g. box width 0.5, height 0.4, depth 0.02), AnchorEntity(world: transform), apply the same 90° X rotation to the frame entity so it lies flat, addChild, addAnchor.

VIEWMODEL:
- Use @Observable or ObservableObject (with @Published) for AR state.
- If using @StateObject, the ViewModel MUST conform to ObservableObject.
- Pass the ViewModel to the UIViewRepresentable via a @Binding or through the Coordinator.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- NEVER forget `import UIKit` in files using UIView, UIViewRepresentable, UIColor, UIImage, etc.
- NEVER write `func makeUIView(context: UIViewRepresentableContext<Self>)` — just use `Context`.
- NEVER use `Context` without the containing struct conforming to UIViewRepresentable.
- NEVER call `arView.session.run()` in `updateUIView` — it restarts the session every SwiftUI update.
- NEVER use @StateObject with a class that doesn't conform to ObservableObject.
- NEVER assume ARKit works in Simulator — it requires a physical device with camera. Provide a fallback UI for simulator.

NSAttributedString in AR/RealityKit: Never use .foregroundStyle, .fontStyle, or .backgroundStyle as NSAttributedString.Key values — these do not exist and will cause a compiler error. The correct keys are .foregroundColor (takes a UIColor), .font (takes a UIFont), .backgroundColor (takes a UIColor). This applies to any SCNText, floating labels, or measurement text rendered in AR space.

AR Ruler / measurement apps: Never place measurement points randomly on tap position alone. The correct pattern is: (1) Show a persistent crosshair or reticle in the exact center of the screen at all times using a SwiftUI overlay. (2) On each tap, place the measurement point at the world position that the center reticle is currently aimed at — use a raycast from the screen center (CGPoint(x: arView.bounds.midX, y: arView.bounds.midY)) not from the tap location. (3) First tap sets point A, second tap sets point B, then show the measurement. (4) Render the two endpoints as small flat discs or dots (not 3D spheres/marbles) so they don't obscure the measurement line. (5) Show the measurement label floating above the midpoint of the line, not overlapping it. (6) Show a Reset button to clear and start over.

MAIN ACTOR ISOLATION: When using ARKit delegates, UIKit coordinators, or any class that implements delegate protocols AND accesses @Published/@Observable properties: (1) Mark the entire Coordinator or delegate class with @MainActor if it accesses @MainActor-isolated properties. (2) Alternatively, wrap property access in MainActor.run { } blocks. (3) ARSession delegate methods that update UI-bound state must dispatch to the main actor. (4) This also applies to CLLocationManagerDelegate, MKMapViewDelegate, and similar UIKit delegate patterns used in SwiftUI via UIViewRepresentable. Without this, you get "main actor-isolated property cannot be referenced from a nonisolated context".
antiPatterns: Forgetting `import UIKit` in files using UIView or UIViewRepresentable — causes 'cannot find type UIView in scope'
Writing explicit UIViewRepresentableContext<Self> instead of just Context
Calling arView.session.run() in updateUIView — restarts the AR session on every SwiftUI update
Using @StateObject with a class that doesn't conform to ObservableObject
Assuming ARKit works in Simulator — no camera access, must provide fallback
Using only .horizontal plane detection when the app needs walls — use planeDetection = [.horizontal, .vertical] for wall/window detection

Trying to paint the real camera feed instead of overlaying a colored plane entity — add a thin ModelEntity with SimpleMaterial on an AnchorEntity(world: transform) with 90° X rotation so it lies flat on the wall
Showing "wall painted" in UI but not adding the wall ModelEntity to the scene — you must call arView.scene.addAnchor(anchor) after anchor.addChild(wallEntity) or nothing appears
Using a tiny box (e.g. generateBox(size: 0.1)) for wall paint — use at least 2m x 2m (e.g. generateBox(width: 2, height: 2, depth: 0.01)) so the paint patch is visible on the wall
Using alignment: .any when raycasting for wall paint — use alignment: .vertical so the entity aligns to the wall; .any can return horizontal planes and cause the paint to float in the room
Raycasting for wall paint with allowing: .estimatedPlane — use allowing: .existingPlaneGeometry with alignment: .vertical for reliable wall hits

Using AnchorEntity(plane: .vertical) for wall paint — it can appear as a floating horizontal shelf. Use AnchorEntity(world: result.worldTransform) and rotate the wall entity 90° around X: wallEntity.transform.rotation = simd_quatf(angle: .pi/2, axis: SIMD3(1,0,0)) so the flat face points toward the camera
Using ARSCNView instead of ARView + RealityKit — use ARView with RealityKit for SwiftUI-era AR; ARSCNView is SceneKit-based and not the preferred path
